segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Good morning everyone.
segments.zh: 大家早上好。我叫珍妮特·帕克，是人力资源经理。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: My name's Janet Parker and I'm the human resources manager.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: We're very happy to welcome you to your new apprenticeship.
segments.zh: 非常高兴欢迎大家来参加这次新的学徒培训。希望接下来的六个月对你们来说是一段积极而愉快的经历。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: I hope that the next six months will be a positive and enjoyable experience for you.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: I'd like to start with some general advice about being an apprentice.
segments.zh: 我想先给大家讲一些当学徒的总体建议。你们大多数人几乎没有在大机构里工作的经验，所以头一周左右可能会相当有挑战。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Most of you have very little or no experience of working for a big organisation and the first week or so may be quite challenging.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: There will be a lot of new information to take in but don't worry too much about trying to remember everything.
segments.zh: 会有很多新信息要吸收，但不用太担心去记住每一件事。重要的是：如果拿不准该怎么做，就去问别人——你会发现你的主管很好相处，不介意给你讲解或帮你一把。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: The important thing is to check with someone if you're not sure what to do – you'll find your supervisor is very approachable and won't mind explaining things or helping you out.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: You're here to learn so make the most of that opportunity.
segments.zh: 你们是来学习的，所以要充分利用这个机会。第一周你们会在不同部门待一待，所以要尽量多和别人聊他们的工作——你会交到新朋友，还能了解到很多有用的信息。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: You'll be spending time in different departments during your first week so make an effort to talk to as many people as possible about their work – you'll make some new friends and find out lots of useful information.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: As well as having a supervisor, you'll each be assigned a mentor.
segments.zh: 除了有主管，你们每个人还会被指派一位导师。这位导师是最近刚完成学徒培训的人，你们每周会和他/她见一次面。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: This person will be someone who's recently completed an apprenticeship and you'll meet with them on a weekly basis.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Their role is to provide help and support throughout your apprenticeship.
segments.zh: 导师的职责是在整个学徒期给你提供帮助和支持。当然，这并不是说他们会替你做工作——相反，他们会问你到目前为止已经达成了哪些目标，同时帮你找出需要改进的地方。你还可以和他们聊聊你更长远的抱负。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Of course, this doesn't mean they'll actually do any of your work for you – instead they'll be asking you about what goals you've achieved so far, as well as helping you to identify any areas for improvement.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: You can also discuss your more long-term ambitions with them as well.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Now I just want to run through a few company policies for our apprenticeship scheme with you...
segments.zh: 现在我想跟大家快速过一遍我们学徒计划的几条公司规定……最重要的是网络。作为工作的一部分，你们会在网上做一些调研，所以你们上网当然是不限量的，但请不要把它用于私人用途——私事你们可以用自己的手机。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Most importantly, the internet.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: As part of your job you'll be doing some research online so obviously you'll have unlimited access for that but please don't use it for personal use – you'll have your own phones for that.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Some of you have already asked me about flexible working.
segments.zh: 你们有些人已经问过我弹性工作的事。在三个月试用期结束后，你们中有些人会符合条件——但这要看你在哪个部门以及你个人的具体情况。所以请不要以为你会自动获准这样做。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: After your probationary three-month period – some of you will be eligible for this – but it will depend on which department you're in and what your personal circumstances are.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: So please don't assume you'll automatically be permitted to do this.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: I want to make sure there's no confusion about our holiday policy.
segments.zh: 我想确保大家对我们的休假规定没有误解。除了法定公共假日之外，我们要求你们在六个月学徒期结束之前不要预订任何假期。只有在生病时才可以请假。如果这会给你带来困难，请跟你的主管说。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Apart from any statutory public holidays we ask that you don't book any holidays until after your six-month apprenticeship has finished.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Time off should only be taken if you are unwell.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Please speak to your supervisor if this is going to be a problem.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: You'll be expected to work a 40-hour week but there may be opportunities to do overtime during busy periods.
segments.zh: 你们每周要工作40小时，但在繁忙时段可能会有加班的机会。虽然不强制你们加班，但这可能是很宝贵的经验——所以如果条件允许，我们建议你们接下来。当然，我们理解大家在工作之外也有别的事要顾，所以如果有时你抽不开身，也不用担心。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Although you're not required to do this, it can be a valuable experience – so we advise you to take it up if possible.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Obviously, we understand that people do have commitments outside work, so don't worry if there are times when you are unavailable.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: As you know, we don't have a formal dress code here – you may wear casual clothes as long as they're practical – and the only restriction for shoes we have is on high heels for health and safety reasons.
segments.zh: 如大家所知，我们这里没有正式的着装规定——只要衣服实用，你们可以穿休闲装——鞋子方面唯一的限制是出于健康和安全考虑不能穿高跟鞋。像运动鞋这样舒适的鞋子更合适。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Comfortable shoes like trainers are preferable.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: There's a heavily subsidised canteen on site where you can get hot meals or salads cheaply.
segments.zh: 厂区里有一个大额补贴的食堂，你们可以在那儿便宜地吃到热餐或沙拉。也提供零食和饮料——所以我们决定实行不允许自带午餐的规定。这一方面是为了鼓励在单位健康饮食，另一方面是为了防止大家在工位上吃东西，那样不卫生。

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: Snacks and drinks are also provided – so we've decided to introduce a no packed lunch policy.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: This is partly to encourage healthy eating at work and partly to stop people from eating at their workstation, which is unhygienic.

segments.speaker: MANAGER
segments.en: OK moving on to ...

questions.title: Questions 11 and 12
instructions: Which TWO pieces of advice for the first week of an apprenticeship does the manager give?

items.prompt: Which TWO pieces of advice for the first week of an apprenticeship does the manager give? (first answer)

pairs.p: talk to as many people as possible
pairs.note: 多和人聊天、交朋友 = get to know colleagues，选 A
结交新同事朋友，对应 A

paraphrase.explain: make an effort to talk to as many people as possible / make new friends → A get to know colleagues。

items.prompt: Which TWO pieces of advice for the first week of an apprenticeship does the manager give? (second answer)

pairs.p: check with someone if you're not sure what to do
pairs.note: 拿不准就去问别人 = ask questions，选 C

paraphrase.explain: The important thing is to check with someone if you're not sure what to do → C ask lots of questions。

questions.title: Questions 13 and 14
instructions: Which TWO things does the manager say mentors can help with?

items.prompt: Which TWO things does the manager say mentors can help with? (first answer)

pairs.note: 谈长远抱负 = 做职业规划，选 B

paraphrase.explain: You can discuss your more long-term ambitions with them → B making career plans。

items.prompt: Which TWO things does the manager say mentors can help with? (second answer)

pairs.p: what goals you've achieved so far
pairs.note: 回顾已达成的目标 = 检视进展，选 E

paraphrase.explain: asking you about what goals you've achieved so far → E reviewing progress。

questions.title: Questions 15–20
instructions: What does the manager say about each of the following aspects of the company policy for apprentices?
Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 15–20.
C It is against the rules.

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: please don't use it for personal use
pairs.note: 工作可用、私用不行 = 有限制，选 B

paraphrase.explain: unlimited access for work 但 don't use it for personal use → B 有限制。

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: it will depend on which department you're in and what your personal circumstances are
pairs.note: 视部门与个人情况而定，非人人可享 = 有限制，选 B

paraphrase.explain: eligible 但 depend on department/circumstances，不自动允许 → B 有限制。

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: we ask that you don't book any holidays until after your six-month apprenticeship has finished
pairs.note: 学徒期内不许订假期 = 违反规定，选 C

paraphrase.explain: don't book any holidays until after your six-month apprenticeship → C 违规。

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: we advise you to take it up if possible
pairs.note: 建议大家尽量加班 = 鼓励，选 A

pairs.p: it can be a valuable experience
pairs.note: 称加班是宝贵经验，语气正面对应 A

paraphrase.explain: it can be a valuable experience – so we advise you to take it up → A 鼓励。

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: Comfortable shoes like trainers are preferable
pairs.note: 运动鞋更可取 preferable，语气鼓励，选 A

paraphrase.explain: Comfortable shoes like trainers are preferable → A 鼓励。

options: C It is against the rules.

pairs.p: we've decided to introduce a no packed lunch policy
pairs.note: 推行不许自带午餐的规定 = 违规，选 C

paraphrase.explain: a no packed lunch policy → C 违规。
